perturbation.original_text: In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; (b) clearly identified as Proprietary Information at the time of its disclosure by each page thereof being marked with an appropriate legend, in bold faced print, indicating that the information is deemed proprietary by the Applicant; and (c) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.changed_text: In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Company to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; (b) clearly identified as Proprietary Information at the time of its disclosure by each page thereof being marked with an appropriate legend, in bold faced print, indicating that the information is deemed proprietary by the Applicant; and (c) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Applicant' with 'Company' in the first line of this section, while maintaining 'Applicant' in subsequent references, creates a conflict. Now, the section starts by referring to the 'Company' disclosing information, but the requirements for protecting that information still reference actions by the 'Applicant'. This introduces uncertainty about who is responsible for meeting those requirements. It is also contradictory since both terms (Applicant, Company) are being used for the same entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In order for the Applicant's Proprietary Information to be protected as described herein, the Proprietary Information must be delivered in written form as discussed in Paragraph 2 above to: American Registry for Internet Numbers Attn: Business Office PO Box 232290 Centreville, VA 20120 USA
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Applicant' with 'Company' in the first line of Paragraph 2. However, the rest of Paragraph 2 and the Contradicted Text (Paragraph 2 as well) still refer to 'Applicant'. This creates a misaligned terminology contradiction because the contract uses both 'Applicant' and 'Company' to refer to the same entity without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations about whose Proprietary Information is being protected and who must deliver it.

perturbation.original_text: ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.changed_text: ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Customer in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.explanation: The paragraph begins discussing the customer's authorization, but then goes on to discuss information received from the applicant and the applicant's written approval. This creates a direct conflict in who has the right to authorize disclosure. It is contradictory since both terms (Applicant, Customer) are being used for the same entity, authorization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Customer in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Applicant' with 'Customer' in the phrase 'specifically identified by Customer in writing'. However, the text continues to refer to 'information received from the Applicant' and 'written approval of the Applicant'. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity, as the same paragraph refers to both 'Customer' and 'Applicant' as having authorization rights, contradicting the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not be modified or amended, except in a written instrument executed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not be modified or amended, except in a written instrument executed by the signatories.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'parties' to 'signatories' introduces potential ambiguity. While the parties to the agreement are presumably the signatories, this isn't explicitly stated and could lead to confusion if a signatory isn't a formal party to the agreement (e.g., an authorized agent). The definition of 'party' would need to be consistent to avoid the contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement may not be assigned or otherwise transferred by either party in wh ole or in part without the express prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not unreasonably be withheld. This consent requirement shall not apply in the event either party shall change its corporate name or merge with another corporation. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall benefit and be binding upon the successors and assigns of the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'parties' to 'signatories' introduces ambiguity as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'party' and their successors and assigns. If a 'signatory' is not explicitly a 'party', this creates a contradiction regarding who is bound by the agreement's terms, particularly concerning assignment and transfer of rights, directly conflicting with the stated definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. This would create a strong in-text contradiction because it is no longer clear who must consent and who is bound by the terms.

perturbation.original_text: THE AMERICAN REGISTRY FOR INTERNET NUMBERS (ARIN) Name: Signed: Date: Title: Address: PO Box 232290 , Centreville, VA 20120 USA Fax: (703) 997-8708 APPLICANT Company Name: Org-ID (if known): Authorized Signer (print) : Ticket No. (if applicable): Signed: Date: Title: E-Mail: Address: Phone: Fax:
perturbation.changed_text: THE AMERICAN REGISTRY FOR INTERNET NUMBERS (ARIN) Name: Signed: Date: Title: Address: PO Box 232290 , Centreville, VA 20120 USA Fax: (703) 997-8708 CLIENT Company Name: Org-ID (if known): Authorized Signer (print) : Ticket No. (if applicable): Signed: Date: Title: E-Mail: Address: Phone: Fax:
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'APPLICANT' to 'CLIENT' at the signature block, while maintaining 'Applicant' in the body of the document, results in a conflict. The signature block specifies the 'Client', but the document's obligations and rights still reference 'Applicant.' This raises the question of whether the 'Client' and 'Applicant' are the same entity, and whether the signature legally binds the intended 'Applicant' entity. It is also contradictory since both terms (Applicant, Client) are being used for the same entity, authorization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Proprietary Information" shall be defined as and limited to, (a) network engineering plans, including subnets, and host counts, and hosts per subnet with projected utilization rates and associated confidence levels of those projections for one and two years in the future; (b) deployment schedules for the network, including major milestones for each subnet; (c) network topology diagrams originated by the Applicant, not previously published or otherwise disclosed to the general public, not previously available without restriction to ARIN or others, and not normally furnished to others without compensation; and (d) such other information which the Applicant desires to protect against unrestricted disclosure or competitive use, and which the parties mutually agree shall be furnished pursuant to this Non-Disclosure Agreement and which is app ropriately identified as being proprietary when initially furnished to ARIN.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'APPLICANT' to 'CLIENT' in the signature block creates a contradiction with the term 'Applicant' used in the contradicted text, specifically in the definition of "Proprietary Information". This misalignment introduces ambiguity regarding which entity's information is covered by the Non-Disclosure Agreement, as the document now references both 'Applicant' and 'Client' without clarifying if they are the same. This violates the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, which includes using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
